info.name: Flight Ops API
info.description:
Three folders:

1. "OpenSky API (direct)" — calls OpenSky's own /states/all endpoint directly using the OAuth2 client-credentials flow, to verify your OpenSky credentials work independent of this backend.
2. "FlightOps API (local)" — calls this project's own /aircraft endpoints. No auth required — this API authenticates to OpenSky internally; it does not require a bearer token from callers.
3. "SignalR Hub (Live Feed)" — documentation only, no runnable request inside it. Postman's collection JSON format has no WebSocket request type at all (confirmed platform limitation, not a config issue — see https://github.com/postmanlabs/postman-app-support/issues/11252), so a WebSocket connection can't be pre-built into this file. Open that folder's description for the exact manual steps (New > WebSocket Request in Postman, then the handshake/invocation messages to send).

SETUP:
1. Collection ▸ Variables tab — set the CURRENT VALUE column (not just Initial) for `clientId` and `clientSecret` with your real OpenSky API client credentials. Both are typed `secret`, so they're masked in the UI, but note that Postman does NOT strip secret-type values from an exported collection file — don't commit real values into this file.
2. `openSkyToken` is set automatically by the "OpenSky API (direct)" folder's pre-request script — it mints a fresh token before each request in that folder. You don't need to set it yourself.
3. `baseUrl` defaults to this API's local HTTP profile (http://localhost:5273). If you run the HTTPS profile instead, either switch `baseUrl` to https://localhost:7173 or disable SSL certificate verification in Postman ▸ Settings (the dev cert is self-signed).
4. `lamin`/`lomin`/`lamax`/`lomax` default to a bounding box over Switzerland — a commonly used OpenSky example region with reliably dense air traffic. `icao24` defaults to a real aircraft's address in valid 6-hex-character format.

exec: console.warn('clientId/clientSecret collection variables are empty — set them in the Variables tab (Current Value) before running requests in this folder.');

request.description: Direct call to OpenSky's /states/all for the default bbox. Expect 200 with a `states` array. This costs OpenSky quota — see the design doc for the credit-cost rules.
Direct call to OpenSky for a single aircraft. Expect 200 with `states` either containing one entry (if currently airborne/tracked) or null.
Expect 200 with an AircraftSnapshot body ({ aircraft: [...], stale: false }). Backed by DirectSnapshotProvider or CachedSnapshotProvider depending on the OpenSky:RateLimitAware setting.
lamin > lamax — an inverted box. Expect 400 with a ValidationProblem body from FluentValidation.
Expect 200 with the aircraft's current state if tracked, otherwise 404 (still a valid-format icao24, just not currently airborne).
Not a 6-character hex address. Expect 400 with a ValidationProblem body — rejected before any call to OpenSky.
Valid 6-hex-character format but not a real/tracked aircraft. Expect 404.

item.name: SignalR Hub (Live Feed) — documentation only, see description
